proof_stages.label: Adjoints of simple polytopes
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines the residual arrangement of the facet hyperplanes of a simple polytope and the adjoint hypersurface as the unique degree k-d-1 hypersurface vanishing on it.

proof_stages.label: Polygon adjoints and the special heptagon map
proof_stages.summary: For k-gons, the adjoint is a plane curve of degree k-3; its real topology is hyperbolic with maximal nested ovals, and dimension counting singles out the heptagon adjoint map as generically finite of degree 864.

proof_stages.label: Rational polypoles and their adjoint maps
proof_stages.summary: The polygon construction is generalized to planar regions bounded by rational curves, with residual nodes replacing residual hyperplane intersections and a finite-list dimension count for generically finite adjoint maps.

proof_stages.label: Positive geometries and canonical forms
proof_stages.summary: The speaker recalls the recursive definition of a positive geometry and its canonical meromorphic form, including simple poles on boundary divisors and residues equal to lower-dimensional canonical forms.

proof_stages.label: Adjoints as canonical numerators
proof_stages.summary: Residue computations show that the adjoint numerator cancels spurious poles, yielding canonical forms for polytopes and for quasi-regular rational polypoles, while related planar examples clarify the limits of interpolation-only adjoints.

proof_stages.label: Dual-volume and scattering-amplitude bridge
proof_stages.summary: For polytopes the canonical function is interpreted as the volume of a polar dual, and in associahedral examples this volume expansion matches sums over Feynman diagrams in biadjoint scalar theory.

proof_stages.label: Amplituhedron setup in G(2,4)
proof_stages.summary: The amplituhedron family considered in the talk is built from a totally positive matrix as the image of the positive Grassmannian G(2,n) in G(2,4), generalizing cyclic polytopes and motivating the positive-geometry problem.

proof_stages.label: Schubert residual arrangement and amplituhedron adjoint theorem
proof_stages.summary: Using the boundary description of Lukowski-Parisi-Williams, the residual arrangement is identified as a union of one-dimensional Schubert varieties, and a unique degree n-4 adjoint gives the canonical form proving these generic amplituhedra are positive geometries.
